Castel barred ICE arrests at 3 NYC immigration courts May 19. Nassau Supreme Court upheld 287(g); NYCLU appealed May 26 (likely mooted by budget ban). Batavia 745+ over 650 capacity, no announced expansion.

OH.notes: 6 ICE-contract facilities ($13.2M+ FY); 287g 0->20 agreements but ~half signing chiefs gone/on leave (Statehouse News May 11); Butler assault lawsuit (Tenelanda, filed May 18) + weekly cancel-contract campaign; statewide week of action May 23-30; OIA report 7,756 detained Jan25-Mar26, <5% violent; new bill to bar civil arrests in sensitive locations (May 13); NEOCC stabbing Jan 30.

OK.notes: 287(g) expanded to 28+ agencies (from 3 pre-2025); Gov. Stitt signed state-level agreements Feb 18/25 2025; DHS Sept 2025 reimbursement + bonuses (~$175M/yr potential). ICE detention at Diamondback/Watonga, Cimarron/Cushing, Kay County/Newkirk + county jails. OKC warehouse blocked, Choctaw Nation purchase.

OR.notes: Sanctuary since 1987 but 1,100 arrests. Operation Black Rose, Palantir ELITE. Lane County sheriff backdoor. Newport fight. Deep.

PA.notes: Moshannon Valley (Clearfield, GEO, 1876 beds, largest in NE) contract expires 2026 — closure fight, April hunger strike, 3 deaths incl Chaofeng Ge suicide (May 27 AP), Lee/Deluzio visit May 28. Phila ICE Out: Parker signed 6/7 May 7, mask ban became law unsigned, effective July. Pittsburgh City Council Bill 286 (Apr 7) + May 6 staging/warrant bills. Berks/Schuylkill warehouses still blocked by DEP, EHB appeal through Nov. 287g constable backdoor paused.

SC.notes: SLED Immigration Enforcement Unit doubled to 82 agents (+261% ops 2025); H.4764 stalled in Senate Judiciary, died at May 14 session end (not in sine die); Red Oak Mobile Park (West Columbia/Lexington) street raid Apr 22; 53 sponsors refused accountability Qs.

SD.notes: Operation Prairie Thunder (Jul 2025-ongoing), 5 state 287(g) agreements, National Guard ICE paperwork support, Minnehaha+Pennington county jails as primary/secondary holding, Drumgoon Dairy audit (38 fired), Madison workplace raids, courthouse arrest/mistrial, 295 ICE arrests Jan-Oct 2025

TN.notes: HB2219 signed ~May 10 2026 (first statewide 287g mandate, all 95 sheriffs by Jan 1 2027). McMinnville/Warren Co unmarked-vehicle ICE sweep May 27 (6+ detained); Ooltewah teen Garzon Romero detained at Chattanooga check-in May 18; Memphis Safe Task Force 8818 arrests + DEA fatal shooting May 12; Mason WTDF ODO inspection Mar 10-12. New: warren-county entry. Updated 6 existing.

TX.notes: Filled gap counties: Frio/Pearsall (STIPC GEO 1,904 bed, Kai Yin Wong death Oct 2025), Williamson/T.Don Hutto (county pulled out 2018, ICE re-contracted direct w/ CoreCivic, now men), Cameron/Port Isabel (SPC ~1,200, Gamboa medical-neglect death, active expansion RFI), Webb/Laredo updated (Gamboa initial-custody node). SB8 mandates 287g for counties >100k by Dec 1 2026.

UT.notes: SLC $145M mega-center (paused DHS review), 0→11 287(g) in one year, 4 Task Force Model, GEO pre-contract, 4,164 arrests. Deep.

VA.notes: Spanberger (D) escalating clash with ICE: ended state-agency 287(g) Feb 2026, EO restricting ICE on state property May 2026, rescinded Youngkin Augusta prison sale; SB 783 conditions local 287(g) (functional ban). ~11k VA ICE arrests since Jan 2025, most no criminal record. Caroline DF over capacity since Nov 2025; new Riverside Regional Jail (Prince George) staging site (7,344 booked, seeking revenue-driven expansion); Farmville CoreCivic-owned (~685 ADP). ICE eyeing 7 new VA sites per ACLU FOIA. Hanover warehouse + Augusta prison proposals blocked.

VT.notes: VT 2025-2026: 107 detentions (10x increase), state DOC MOU $185/day (NWSCF Swanton + CRCF S. Burlington), Pleasant Valley dairy raid (8 arrested Apr 2025, 3 deported), Hardwick construction arrests (9 arrested Sep 2025, 7 to LA), South Burlington standoff (Mar 2026, botched raid, all 3 released), Mahdawi/Tendo high-profile cases, Burlington sanctuary EO, H.298 to terminate DOC-ICE cooperation, strong judicial pushback

WA.notes: NWIPC bridge contract (9M, to Oct 27 2026) confirmed to embed 1,635-bed expansion + weaker 2025 NDS (UW "Scrapping the Standards" May 5). DOH inspectors still blocked by GEO; Ferguson/Brown preliminary-injunction hearing held May 26 (ruling pending); L&I won permanent injunction. HB 1232 oversight signed (covers Martin Hall youth facility); HB 2464 died in Senate; tax/fine bills failed. Yakima Valley ~20 arrests in one week May 2026.

WY.notes: WY full research: 5 county 287(g)s (Campbell, Carbon, Laramie, Natrona, Sweetwater), WHP statewide 287(g), county jails as Aurora overflow (Natrona 44 detainees $95/day, Laramie 257 detainees $120/day, Sweetwater 30 beds $3M projected, Uinta 25 beds $120/day), failed CoreCivic 1000-bed Evanston facility (2017-2020), Nov 2025 trucker operation (40 deportations), ICE ERO office in Casper + planned Gillette/Rock Springs offices, anti-sanctuary legislation passed, ICE watch hotline launched Jackson Mar 2026
